review_notes: The functions generally map well to the control SI-2-c, which focuses on keeping software and firmware up-to-date. The naming convention is mostly followed, but one function needed a slight adjustment for clarity. The functions are technically accurate and align with Kubernetes security practices.
naming_improvements: Changed k8s_admission_controller_security_patches_applied to k8s_admission_controller_security_patch_enforcement to better describe the function's purpose
title: Flaw Remediation - Install security-relevant software and firmware...
step1_reasoning: The control SI-2-c focuses on ensuring that security-relevant software and firmware are up-to-date, which can be mapped to Kubernetes in several ways. Ensuring nodes are automatically upgraded and patched aligns with maintaining up-to-date software. Image vulnerability scanning ensures that container images do not have known vulnerabilities. Admission controllers can enforce policies that require security patches to be applied before deployment. Keeping the Kubernetes cluster version up-to-date ensures that all components benefit from the latest security patches.
